checks.note: `checklist_items` is typed as `dict[str, ChecklistItem] | None` but the function only reads from it (passes to `enrich_check`). The parameter should be `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` to accept the minimal read-only interface.

annotations.message: Use `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` — function only reads, never mutates this dict

checks.note: `checklist_items: dict[str, ChecklistItem] | None` accepts a mutable concrete dict, but the body only passes it to `enrich_check` which reads via `Mapping`-compatible access. Narrowing to `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` expresses the true dependency.

annotations.message: Accept `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` — only key-based reads are performed

checks.note: The `if stage in (...): ... elif stage == ...:` block dispatches on string literal values. A `match stage:` with `case "changeset" | "file":` and `case "symbol":` expresses the dispatch intent more directly and aligns with the `match`-based style used elsewhere in the file (e.g., `_extract_symbol_entries`, `target_sort_key`).

annotations.message: Replace if/elif stage dispatch with `match stage:` — intent is structural dispatch on a string literal
